a.style: font-family:'Playfair Display', Georgia, 'Times New Roman', 'Liberation Serif', serif

text.tspan: Countries using the System of Environmental-Economic Accounting,
The System of Environmental-Economic Accounting (SEEA) is a framework that integrates economic and environmental
data, to provide a more comprehensive view of the relationships between the economy and the environment. Shown are
all the countries that have compiled SEEA accounts at least once.
Data source: United Nations Environment Programme, Convention on Biological Diversity and United Nations Statistics Division
Note: The SEEA presents information in physical and monetary terms regarding environmental stocks and flows between the environment
and the economy, as well as economic activity related to the environment.
